content: My team at Stripe recently shipped a new payments feature that processes international transactions in under 200 milliseconds.
I enjoy board games. My favorites are Settlers of Catan and Terraforming Mars. Game night is every other Saturday.
I switched from regular milk to oat milk last year and noticed my digestion improved.
I am left-handed, which sometimes makes using scissors inconvenient.
I play guitar in a band called The Syntax Errors. We perform covers at local bars monthly.
I maintain a detailed Notion document where I track my goals for each quarter.
I did a 10-day silent meditation retreat in 2023. It was transformative for my practice.
My cat Luna loves to sit on my keyboard while I am coding. It is both adorable and frustrating.
I am mildly lactose intolerant. I avoid dairy products when possible.
I recently bought a Honda Civic for weekend road trips. The Tesla is for daily commuting.
I built a custom mechanical keyboard with Holy Panda switches and GMK keycaps.
I completed the John Muir Trail through-hike in 2022. It took three weeks.
I taught a Go programming workshop at a local coding bootcamp last month.
My morning routine includes 15 minutes of journaling. I write stream of consciousness.
I bought my first home in San Francisco in 2021. It is a two-bedroom condo.
My favorite cuisine is Thai food. I love the green curry from a restaurant in the Mission.
I have a standing desk and use it for about 4 hours each day before switching to sitting.
I have traveled to 15 countries so far. Japan and Iceland are my favorites.
I play basketball recreationally on Wednesday evenings at a local gym.
I save 30% of my income each month and track my budget in a detailed spreadsheet.
I have a 27-inch 4K monitor for my home office and a 15-inch laptop for travel.
My favorite podcast is Lex Fridman. The depth of the conversations is unmatched.
I gave a talk on Go concurrency patterns at the SF Gophers meetup in February.
I am reading Clean Code by Robert Martin with my book club. We meet monthly.
I passed the AWS Solutions Architect Associate exam last year. Planning for Professional next.
My team uses feature flags with LaunchDarkly for gradual rollouts of new features.
I have been meditating daily for three years. It has significantly reduced my stress levels.
I journal every evening to reflect on the day. It helps me process my thoughts.
I just bought a new electric car, a Honda e. It is perfect for city driving.
I run with a local running club on Saturday mornings. We meet at the Ferry Building.
I compost all food waste and recycle diligently. Sustainability is important to me.
I have a 1500-day streak on Duolingo learning Japanese. I can read basic hiragana and katakana.
I host movie nights at my place every other Friday. We watch classic films.
I enjoy street photography and take my camera on weekend walks through different neighborhoods.
I have about 500 followers on Twitter where I post about Go programming and tech.
I know Python well and use it for scripting and data analysis alongside Go.
My living room is decorated in a minimalist style with lots of plants.
I built a Chrome extension for organizing bookmarks. It has about 50 users among my friends.
My team at Stripe is working on a project codenamed Mercury. It is a new payments API.
I am an uncle to my brother David's daughter, Sophie. She is 2 years old.
I recently got into home fermentation. My kimchi takes about 3 weeks to prepare.
My favorite board game strategy is to focus on resource monopolization early.
I learned to play Blackbird by The Beatles on my acoustic guitar last week.
My team uses PostgreSQL as our primary database. I have experience with tuning it for high throughput.
I have a personal wiki where I document coding patterns and solutions I discover.
I organized a team offsite in Napa Valley last quarter. We went wine tasting and did brainstorming sessions.
I am learning to cook Thai food at home. My pad thai needs more practice.
I volunteer at a local coding bootcamp teaching Go fundamentals to beginners.
My sister Emma graduated from Stanford with a degree in biology in June 2024.
I recently updated my personal website. It is built with Hugo and hosted on Netlify.
I run a weekly newsletter about Go programming tips. It has 300 subscribers.
I bought a new cast iron skillet and use it almost daily for cooking.
I am learning to play piano. I can play Moonlight Sonata first movement after 6 months of practice.
I use the Headspace app for guided meditation sessions.
My team recently shipped a major performance improvement that reduced API latency by 40%.
I prefer tea over coffee in the evenings. My favorite is chamomile.
I have a vinyl record collection with about 100 records. My rarest is a first pressing of OK Computer.
I co-host a podcast called Bits and Brews where we discuss tech over beers.
I have a mechanical keyboard with Holy Panda switches. The tactile bump is perfect for typing.
Building mechanical keyboards is a hobby of mine. I have soldered three boards so far.
I listen to Huberman Lab for science-based health and wellness information.
I read Nonviolent Communication by Marshall Rosenberg. It changed how I approach difficult conversations.
I run a book club at work. We just finished Clean Code by Robert Martin.
I have been working on my communication skills through Toastmasters for the past year.
I completed a 30-day yoga challenge in March 2024. Now I practice 3 times a week.
I have a small garden on my balcony where I grow tomatoes, herbs, and peppers.
I am a night owl. My most productive coding hours are between 10 PM and midnight.
I have 20/15 vision in my left eye and 20/20 in my right. I wear contact lenses.
I completed a creative writing workshop and wrote a short science fiction story.
I recently attended my sister Emma's graduation ceremony at Stanford.
I have a time-tracking system where I log how I spend each work hour.
I use a 4-3-2 time blocking method: 4 hours deep work, 3 hours meetings, 2 hours email.
My favorite San Francisco restaurant is Zuni Cafe. Their roast chicken is legendary.
I play Settlers of Catan competitively. My best strategy is longest road victory.
I recently won a Catan game with 12 points including longest road and largest army.
I am going on a 2-week vacation to New Zealand in December 2024 to hike the Milford Track.
My rent is $2,800 per month for a two-bedroom apartment in the Mission District.
I code from a local coffee shop a couple of times a week for a change of scenery.
I have a personal project in Go that is a CLI tool for organizing markdown files.
I wrote a CLI that parses markdown frontmatter and generates a table of contents.
I use Neovim as a secondary editor. My config is written in Lua.
I discovered a great Italian restaurant called Tony's Pizza in North Beach.
I walk 10,000 steps daily and track my fitness with a Garmin watch.
I completed a sprint triathlon in September 2023. My time was 3 hours and 15 minutes.
My team at Stripe won the internal hackathon last month with an AI-powered fraud detection tool.
I use GitHub Actions for CI/CD. Our pipeline runs tests in under 5 minutes.
I bike to the office on days I go in. The ride is about 20 minutes from the Mission to SoMa.
I recently visited a great ramen shop in Tokyo called Ichiran. The tonkotsu broth was incredible.
I started a side project building a personal knowledge management tool in Go.
My team does weekly retrospectives every Friday. We share interesting articles or papers we found.
I brew my own kombucha at home. The fermentation takes about 8 days.
My favorite book is Sapiens by Yuval Noah Harari. It changed how I think about human history.
I have a cat named Luna who wakes me up at 6 AM every morning for breakfast.
I went deep-sea fishing in Monterey Bay last month and caught a rockfish.
I prefer listening to lo-fi hip hop or jazz while coding. Lyrics are too distracting.
My favorite season is spring. The weather in San Francisco is perfect for outdoor activities.
I love seeing the cherry blossoms in Golden Gate Park every spring.
I started using a standing desk and it has helped with my posture.
I am 33 years old and feel like I am in the best shape of my life.
My daily standup meeting is at 9:30 AM. It never takes more than 15 minutes.
I run 5K in about 24 minutes. I am working on getting under 22 minutes.
I recently read The Pragmatic Programmer. The section on DRY was eye-opening.
My team uses GitHub Actions for continuous integration and deployment.
I bike to work on nice days. The route from the Mission to SoMa takes about 20 minutes.
I prefer dark mode in all my applications. Light mode strains my eyes.
My favorite movie is The Shawshank Redemption. I watch it at least once a year.
I visited Tokyo and loved the ramen at Ichiran in Shibuya. The self-serve booth format was unique.
I attended a Scala training course in 2020. It was a two-week intensive program.
I have 20/20 vision in both eyes. I have never needed glasses.
I wake up at 6:30 AM every day. On weekends I read for 30 minutes before getting out of bed.
I listen to the Lex Fridman podcast during my commute. The episodes are usually 2-3 hours long.
I gave a Go workshop at the SF Gophers meetup in January. About 40 developers attended.
I am reading Designing Data-Intensive Applications. It is excellent for understanding distributed systems.
I started a personal blog at alexcodes.dev. My first post was about concurrent Go patterns.
Dolores Park is my favorite place in San Francisco on a sunny afternoon.
I am training for my first marathon. My goal time is under 4 hours.
I prefer dark roast coffee and drink it black with two sugars.
I use Obsidian for all my notes. I have a Zettelkasten-style linking structure.
I lead a team of 6 engineers at Stripe. My manager's name is Elena.
My grandmother taught me her secret pasta sauce recipe. It uses San Marzano tomatoes.
My team does retrospectives every two weeks using a Start/Stop/Continue format.
I attended GopherCon 2023 and gave a lightning talk on error handling patterns.
I have a 100-day GitHub streak going. I started it in January 2024.
I enjoy playing Settlers of Catan. My favorite strategy is to monopolize ore and wheat.
I am going to New Zealand in December. We plan to hike the Milford Track.
I pay $2,800 per month in rent. It is a lot but worth it for the location.
I like to code from different coffee shops around the Mission District.
I built a side project that organizes my markdown notes into a wiki.
I use a CLI tool I wrote in Go that parses markdown frontmatter.
My Neovim config is about 500 lines of Lua. I tweak it regularly.
Tony's Pizza in North Beach has the best margherita pizza in the city.
I walk 10,000 steps every day. My Garmin watch helps me stay accountable.
I finished my first sprint triathlon in September 2023. The swim was the hardest part.
My team's hackathon project was an AI tool that detects fraudulent transactions in real-time.
Our CI pipeline uses GitHub Actions and runs about 200 tests in under 5 minutes.
I commute by bike on Tuesdays and Thursdays when I go to the office.
I loved the tonkotsu ramen at Ichiran in Tokyo. The broth was incredibly rich.
My side project is a personal knowledge management tool built with Go and SQLite.
Every Friday my team shares one interesting article or paper we read that week.
I started brewing kombucha last month. The first batch was a bit too vinegary.
Sapiens by Yuval Noah Harari is my favorite non-fiction book.
Luna the cat is 5 years old. Her birthday is in June.
I caught a rockfish on a deep-sea fishing trip in Monterey Bay last month.
I cannot code with music that has lyrics. Lo-fi hip hop is my go-to coding soundtrack.
Spring in San Francisco means cherry blossoms in Golden Gate Park.
My standing desk has helped reduce my back pain from sitting all day.
I turned 33 in March 2025. I celebrated with friends at a nice restaurant in the Mission.
Our standup is at 9:30 AM sharp. We use a round-robin format.
My current 5K personal record is 23 minutes and 45 seconds.
The Pragmatic Programmer taught me to be more intentional about my coding practices.
